selection_reason: WP2 PIT dataset manifest was source-contract-ready; registry/advisory serving metadata hardening is the next unblocked source-only dependency before advisory DreamEngine and model-serving work. Runtime/order-capable paths remain blocked by expired standing Demo authorization, so the selected scope is advisory-only source contract and tests.
preconditions: WP1 shortened-chain concern closed by E2/E4/QA review
WP2 PIT dataset manifest committed and state advanced
runtime/order-capable work remains blocked by expired standing Demo authorization
registry/advisory metadata hardening is explicitly after ProofPacket and PIT gates
dirty worktree classified and unrelated memory/* left untouched
acceptance: Define registry_serving_contract_v1 schema, validator, canonical hash, extractor, and attachment helper.
Contract is advisory-only and requires not_authority=true, symlink_authority=false, promotion_serving_ready=false, serving_mode=advisory_only, and pit_dataset_manifest_v1 lineage.
Contract requires dataset, label, feature, split, leakage, serving config, policy, artifact_hashes q10/q50/q90, quantile_trio q10/q50/q90, and contract_hash fields.
Contract rejects unknown top-level fields, malformed hashes, partial quantile trios, non-string policy fields, and authority aliases.
Python model registry binds provided contract artifact_hashes to actual local ONNX artifact sha256 before DB registration.
Python model registry raises fail-loud registry_trio_persistence_incomplete when contract-backed trio registration partially persists.
Rust registry validator enforces advisory boundary fields and compares resolved row artifact_sha256 with the resolved quantile contract hash.
Rust direct reload path remains fail-closed until registry-authorized serving contract integration exists.
Rust and FastAPI build capabilities do not advertise direct reload_edge_predictor availability even if ORT or stale IPC claims it.
Focused Python, expanded ML, Rust registry, Rust reload, Rust capabilities, rustfmt, py_compile, and diff-check verification pass.
No runtime, DB, exchange, MCP, secret, order/probe, Cost Gate, deploy, live, or mainnet action is performed.
kill_gates: STOP_TEST if focused, expanded, Rust, rustfmt, or diff-check verification fails
STOP_EVIDENCE if registry serving metadata cannot be machine-checkable or artifact-bound
STOP_BOUNDARY if runtime/order/private/MCP/live action is required
